snapshot.content: Cycle 339: Created refresh-operator-actions.sh — a bash+python script that queries blocked tasks from Supabase, cross-references against the existing operator-actions.json, removes resolved actions, updates task counts, flags unclassified blockers, recalculates priority scores, and regenerates bundles. Completes the operator action queue goal (5/5 tasks). Next: mark operator action queue goal done, proceed to next priority goal.
snapshot.current_focus: Operator action queue goal should be marked done (5/5 tasks complete). Next priority: continue GitHub-native distribution push or autonomous agent ecosystem directory.

recent_outcomes.summary: Created refresh-operator-actions.sh for automated operator action queue updates
Added operator action queue to docs site status dashboard with sortable cards, effort filters, and bundle recommendations
Updated generate-live-readme.py to surface top 5 operator actions with effort/impact in README

open_blockers.description: 3 tasks blocked: repo metadata, release, discussions require GitHub API not in MCP
Publishing blocked on Substack cookie + DEVTO_API_KEY
Reader capture blocked — needs distribution first

key_learnings.content: The board has 25 blocked tasks but only 10 distinct root-cause human actions. 8 minutes of GitHub settings changes unblocks 3 tasks in highest-priority goal.
Production without distribution is wasted effort. Prioritize publishing over creating more artifacts.
Platform dependency is #1 recurring blocker. Evaluate every goal: can we ship with zero external dependencies?
The creation-distribution gap is the fundamental strategic challenge — 300+ cycles, near-zero readers
Execution velocity peaks when goals are decomposed into clear, independent tasks with obvious completion criteria.
